node_2: larger mim pre-trained model as teacher
edge: TinyMIM adopts a larger MIM pre-trained model as the teacher.
TinyMIM's objective is to train the randomly initialized student by mimicking the target produced by the teacher in a knowledge distillation manner with a smaller ViT as the student.
In this work, MAE is adopted as the MIM model due to its popularity and simplicity.
After pre-training, the TinyMIM pre-trained model can be transferred to various downstream tasks.
Factors that may affect TinyMIM pre-training include distillation target, input, and target block as described in different sections.

edge: Given an input image x, it is divided into N non-overlapping patches and mapped into patch embeddings F0 ∈ RN×D using a linear projection layer.

M Relations are the concatenation of various Q, K, V matrices.

Knowledge distillation is focused on transferring knowledge from teacher model ft to student model fs while optimizing the student model.
The loss of class token distillation is formulated as L = LKL(cs, ct), where ct and cs represent the class token feature of the teacher and student models respectively.
The feature dimension of the teacher network may be mismatched with the student network, and an extra linear layer is adopted on the output of the student network to match the feature dimension.

The relation distillation loss (Eq. 9) is calculated head by head, thus solving the head misalignment issue before performing relation distillation.
To solve the head misalignment issue, the last block of the student is replaced with an adaptive block, maintaining the original hidden dimension but adjusting the head number to match the teacher.
A teacher network with Mt heads per block and a student network with Ms heads per block are compared based on hidden dimensions and head dimensions for designing the adaptive block.
When training a small model like ViT-S, the teacher has two options: a pre-trained ViT-B and a pre-trained ViT-L, with ViT-L offering higher representation capability.
The pre-trained ViT-L is a good teacher due to its higher representation capability, but there is a capacity gap between ViT-L and ViT-S, leading to poor distillation results.
Sequential distillation strategy is adopted to improve pre-training, selecting a TinyMIM pre-trained ViT-B as the teacher for pre-training a ViT-S model.
